description: Clinical laboratory calculations, biomarker interpretation, and diagnostic thresholds.

facts.context: Calculated serum osmolality uses the formula:
facts.truth: 2 x Na + glucose/18 + BUN/2.8 in mOsm/kg
distractors: 2 x Na + glucose/28 + BUN/1.8 in mOsm/kg
Na + glucose/18 + BUN/2.8 + K in mOsm/kg
2 x Na + glucose/10 + creatinine/2.8 mOsm/kg

facts.context: An elevated osmolal gap (> 10 mOsm/kg) suggests:
facts.truth: unmeasured osmoles present such as toxic alcohols
distractors: severe dehydration without any exogenous substances
laboratory calibration error in sodium measurement
normal physiologic variation requiring no workup

facts.context: AST/ALT ratio > 2:1 with elevated GGT suggests:
facts.truth: alcoholic liver disease as the primary hepatic injury pattern
distractors: nonalcoholic fatty liver disease as the primary pattern
acute viral hepatitis A or B causing hepatocellular damage
biliary obstruction from gallstones causing cholestatic injury

facts.context: High-sensitivity troponin I kinetics after myocardial injury show:
facts.truth: initial rise at 4-6 hours, peak at 12-24 hours after onset
distractors: initial rise at 12-24 hours, peak at 48-72 hours after onset
immediate rise within minutes, peak at 2-4 hours after onset
initial rise at 24-48 hours, peak at 72-96 hours after onset

facts.context: BNP levels in acute dyspnea evaluation indicate:
facts.truth: BNP > 400 pg/mL strongly suggests heart failure as the cause
distractors: BNP > 40 pg/mL strongly suggests heart failure as the cause
BNP > 4000 pg/mL strongly suggests heart failure as the cause
BNP > 400 pg/mL strongly suggests pulmonary embolism instead

facts.context: HbA1c of 6.5% corresponds to an average glucose of:
facts.truth: approximately 140 mg/dL over the preceding 2-3 months
distractors: approximately 100 mg/dL over the preceding 2-3 months
approximately 200 mg/dL over the preceding 2-3 months
approximately 140 mg/dL over the preceding 2-3 weeks

facts.context: Corrected calcium for hypoalbuminemia is calculated as:
facts.truth: measured Ca + 0.8 x (4.0 - albumin) in mg/dL
distractors: measured Ca - 0.8 x (4.0 - albumin) in mg/dL
measured Ca + 1.2 x (3.5 - albumin) in mg/dL
measured Ca + 0.8 x (albumin - 4.0) in mg/dL

facts.context: The delta-delta ratio is calculated and interpreted as:
facts.truth: (AG - 12) / (24 - HCO3); ratio > 2 indicates concurrent metabolic alkalosis
distractors: (AG - 12) / (24 - HCO3); ratio < 1 indicates concurrent metabolic alkalosis
(AG + 12) / (24 + HCO3); ratio > 2 indicates concurrent respiratory acidosis
(24 - HCO3) / (AG - 12); ratio > 2 indicates concurrent metabolic alkalosis

facts.context: For diagnosing pancreatitis, lipase compared to amylase is:
facts.truth: more specific with a longer serum half-life for detection
distractors: less specific but with a much shorter serum half-life
equally specific but only elevated in chronic pancreatitis
more sensitive but normalizes within 2 hours of onset

facts.context: Creatine kinase MB fraction interpretation shows:
facts.truth: CK-MB > 5% of total CK suggests a myocardial source of injury
distractors: CK-MB > 25% of total CK suggests a myocardial source of injury
CK-MB > 5% of total CK suggests a skeletal muscle injury source
CK-MB < 1% of total CK suggests a myocardial source of injury

facts.context: Ferritin in iron deficiency diagnosis shows:
facts.truth: ferritin < 30 ng/mL is diagnostic with 92% sensitivity
distractors: ferritin < 200 ng/mL is diagnostic with 92% sensitivity
ferritin < 30 ng/mL is diagnostic with only 40% sensitivity
ferritin > 30 ng/mL is diagnostic with 92% specificity

facts.context: Primary hyperthyroidism is confirmed by the pattern:
facts.truth: low TSH with elevated free T4 on thyroid function tests
distractors: high TSH with elevated free T4 on thyroid function tests
low TSH with decreased free T4 on thyroid function tests
normal TSH with elevated free T3 on thyroid function tests
